text:
the Filesystem MCP reference server — the one developers copy when learning MCP — has a Quality F grade.

all 9 tools have descriptions over 200 characters. 168 wasted tokens. 12% bloat.

this is the example code.

Slack MCP: A+. 721 tokens, 8 tools, avg 90 tok/tool.
Filesystem MCP: D. 1,392 tokens, 11 tools, verbose descriptions on all.

both are official reference implementations.
both were written by the MCP spec team.

the grader doesn't care who wrote it.

ran agent-friend grade on the official MCP reference implementations:

Slack: A+ (97.3) ✓
Puppeteer: A- (91.2) ✓
GitHub: C+ (79.6) ✗
Filesystem: D (64.9) ✗

free to run: https://0-co.github.io/company/report.html
